tasks.description: Enforce agent input schema validation in executeAgentStep() — validate resolved input against AgentSpec.inputSchema before dispatch, fail step on violation
acceptance_criteria: executeAgentStep validates input against agent.inputSchema when both are present
Step fails with clear error message naming the agent and listing validation errors
Agents without inputSchema skip validation (backward compatible)
Steps providing no input to an agent with required schema fields fail
Test covers: valid input passes, missing required field fails, extra field passes (additionalProperties default)
resumable-analysis.workflow.yaml and pausable-analysis.workflow.yaml fixed to pass all 3 required synthesizer fields (or synthesizer schema relaxed)
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-001. Implemented by subagent (opus). 5 new tests covering all acceptance criteria. validate() from schema-validator called after resolvedInput, before compileAgentSpec. Agents without inputSchema skip validation (backward compat). 619/0 pass/fail across full suite.

tasks.description: Audit and fix all workflow specs that pass incomplete input to schema'd agents — ensure every agent invocation satisfies the agent's input schema
acceptance_criteria: Every workflow step that invokes an agent with an inputSchema passes all required fields
No workflow fails schema validation after task-001 enforcement is added
Decision recorded: for each spec, whether the fix is to add missing input or relax the schema
tasks.notes: Companion to task-001. Must be done simultaneously — enforcement without fixing callers breaks existing workflows.

tasks.description: Identify agent families that share structural patterns and refactor to use template inheritance (extends/blocks)
acceptance_criteria: At least 2 new template families use extends/block composition (beyond existing analyzers/)

Candidate families: investigators (investigator, gap-identifier, gap-resolution-assessor share 'read code → structured findings → validate schema'), planners (plan-creator, plan-decomposer, decomposer share 'analyze input → produce ordered tasks')
Base templates extract common workflow/constraints/success_criteria structure
Specialized templates override only the domain-specific blocks
All existing tests pass — prompt output is semantically equivalent
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-002. Proves that our template composition model works beyond the single analyzer family.

tasks.description: Redesign reusable agents (explorer, structure-analyzer, synthesizer) to accept variable input shapes via optional fields and conditional template rendering
acceptance_criteria: code-explorer accepts optional depth (quick/medium/thorough) and focus_areas fields — template adapts prompt accordingly
synthesizer accepts patterns as optional — template renders 2-channel or 3-channel synthesis based on what's provided
Input schemas updated with optional fields and proper defaults
Task templates use {% if %} conditionals to adapt to present/absent optional fields
Existing workflows continue to work (new fields are optional with sensible defaults)
At least one workflow demonstrates a genuinely different composition (e.g., quick explorer vs thorough explorer)
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-003. Transforms structural repetition into parametric recombination.

tasks.description: Implement ad-hoc single-agent invocation via /workflow run-agent command and tool parameter
acceptance_criteria: /workflow run-agent <name> dispatches a single agent step without a workflow spec
Input provided as JSON string or key=value pairs on the command line
Agent's input schema validates the provided input (if schema exists)
Agent's task template renders with the provided input
Agent's output schema validates the result (if schema exists)
Result displayed in conversation with the same formatting as workflow step results
Tool parameter variant: workflow tool accepts { agent: name, input: {...} } for LLM-driven ad-hoc invocation
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-004. Bridges the gap pi-subagents fills naturally with /run.

tasks.description: Implement worktree isolation option for parallel step execution
acceptance_criteria: parallel: steps accept optional worktree: true field in workflow YAML
When enabled: temp git worktrees created per parallel sub-step, node_modules symlinked
Each agent runs in its own worktree's cwd preserving subdirectory context
After execution: git diff captured per worktree, diffs included in step result
Worktrees cleaned up in finally block (even on failure)
Validation: requires clean git working tree, rejects if dirty
DAG-inferred parallelism also supports worktree flag (step-level config)
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-005. Can adapt pi-subagents' worktree.ts (326 lines, well-structured) as reference implementation.

tasks.description: Implement workflow recursion depth guard for Phase 6 nested workflow invocation
acceptance_criteria: PI_WORKFLOW_MAX_DEPTH env var sets maximum nesting (default 2)
PI_WORKFLOW_DEPTH env var propagated to child processes, incremented per level
Exceeding depth fails the step with clear error message
Configurable via env var before starting pi
Tests cover: depth 0 blocks all, depth 1 allows one level, depth exceeded fails
tasks.notes: Fixes issue-006. Prerequisite for unblocking workflow: step type in Phase 6.
